methods.description: Process for setting up bioassay cages with bees to study the effects of pollen availability on bee behavior and mortality.

steps.description: One hundred emerging bees were transferred to the Plexiglas bioassay cages equipped with transparent and removable sides, ventilation holes, and two syringes of 2.5 mL, which were adapted as feeders to provide a water and sucrose solution (50% w/w).
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer 100 emerging bees to bioassay cages with feeders for water and sucrose solution.

steps.description: A piece of wax was hung in the middle of each cage near the sugar solution and water for the bees to cluster on.
steps.searchable_summary: Hang a piece of wax in each cage for bee clustering.

steps.description: Hoarding bioassay cages were maintained at controlled conditions (34 ± 1°C, 50-70% relative humidity) and kept in the dark to simulate conditions within the colony.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain bioassay cages at controlled temperature and humidity in the dark.

steps.description: Pollen balls were provided ad libitum for the control bees only; these balls were prepared by mixing pollen with honey until a soft, cohesive paste was obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare and provide pollen balls for control bees.

steps.description: Water and sucrose were also provided ad libitum for all the cages.
steps.searchable_summary: Provide water and sucrose solution for all cages.

steps.description: A pollen ball of 10 grams was offered to each cage; this quantity allowed the pollen to be constantly available to the bees.
steps.searchable_summary: Offer 10g pollen ball to each cage.

steps.description: The quantity of pollen consumed by caged bees is determined by the difference between the weight of pollen supplied and left after consumption.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate pollen consumption by weight difference.

steps.description: The diet was provided periodically at 1 or 2 day intervals, and at these time intervals, dead bees were removed and counted.
steps.searchable_summary: Provide diet periodically and count dead bees.

steps.description: The dead bees and the total number of bees in each cage were considered in the estimation of consumption.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate consumption considering dead and total bees.

steps.description: The mortality of the bees was registered until the last bee.
steps.searchable_summary: Register bee mortality until last bee.

methods.description: Setup of different treatment groups for the bee bioassay to study the effects of pollen availability.

steps.description: Three cages each containing 100 newly emerged A. mellifera intermissa or A. mellifera sahariensis worker bees were registered for each of the following treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: Register three cages for each bee treatment group.
Setup Lot 1: A. mellifera sahariensis pollen-deprived.
Setup Lot 2: A. mellifera sahariensis pollen-fed.
Setup Lot 3: A. mellifera intermissa pollen-deprived.
Setup Lot 4: A. mellifera intermissa pollen-fed.

steps.description: For statistical analyses of data, we considered the three repetitions of each treatment as a single lot (one lot consists of three cages that received the same treatment).
steps.searchable_summary: Combine three repetitions of each treatment for statistical analysis.
